message:
fix(codegen): Fix Rat class dunder method dispatch

**Root Cause**: Class instances with __eq__ were converted to PyValue.none
- PyValue.from(Rat) → .none (struct without __base_value__)
- PyValue.none.eql(PyValue.none) → true for ALL comparisons
- assertEqual(Rat(10,1), Rat(20,2)) always passed!

**Fix**:
1. comparison.zig - Remove try wrapping from __eq__/__ne__ calls
   - __eq__ returns bool, not !bool (per MagicMethodReturnTypes)
   - Generated: x.__eq__(y) instead of runtime.toBool(try x.__eq__(y))

2. equality.zig - Call __eq__ on structs with dunder methods
   - pyAnyEql: Check @hasDecl(T, "__eq__") before PyValue conversion
   - pyTupleEql: Same check for struct equality
   - Preserves class instance type info instead of losing to .none

3. assertions.zig - Use runtime.pyAnyEql for generic fallback
   - Changed from PyValue.from().eql() to runtime.pyAnyEql()
   - Handles __eq__ methods on class instances correctly
   - Note: Direct __eq__ calls don't work when expr returns !Rat

**Result**: test_RatTestCase_test_eq now uses proper __eq__ dispatch
**Status**: Still 9/9 Rat tests failing - investigating remaining PyValue issues
